for any particular reading, error in x may 10\%
maximum percentage error in x is $8 \%$.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
In a standard meter bridge experiment, to measure the specific resistance of a wire the following data are found:-
Length $(\mathrm{L})=(1 \pm .01) \mathrm{m}$ measured by a meter scale
Radius of wire $=1 \mathrm{~mm} \pm .01 \mathrm{~mm}$. (measured by screw gauge).
Resistance of wire $(R)=(5 \pm .01) \Omega$
The maximum possible error in the measurement of specific resistance is
(You may use the formula:- $\rho=\frac{\pi r^2}{\mathrm{~L}} R$ )
questions.solution:
$\rho=\frac{3 \cdot 14 \times 10^{-6} \times 5}{1}$
$=15.7 \mu$ ohm metre
$\delta \rho=\rho\left[\frac{2 \delta r}{r}+\frac{\delta \mathrm{L}}{\mathrm{L}}+\frac{\delta \mathrm{R}}{\mathrm{R}}\right]$ ohm metre.
$=15 \cdot 7 \mu\left[2 \times \frac{.01}{1}+\frac{.01}{1}+\frac{.01}{5}\right]$ ohm metre
$\begin{aligned} & =15.7 \mu[\cdot 02+\cdot 01+\cdot 002] \text { ohm }- \text { metre } \\ & =15.7 \times \cdot 032 \mu \Omega \mathrm{m} \\ & =.5024 \mu \Omega \mathrm{m}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: We can compensate backlash error by
options: Measuring the objects in ascending order of their sizes.
Measuring the objects in descending order of their sizes.
It is not possible as it is random error.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
When a piece of wire is held diametrically in a screw gauge [pitch $=1 \mathrm{~mm}$, number of division on circular scale $=100]$. The readings obtained are as shown:

Now if we measure the same with help of vernier callipers [ $1 \mathrm{MSD}=1 \mathrm{~mm}, 10$ divisions of vernier coinciding with 9 divisions of main scale] having a negative zero error of 0.5 mm , then find which of the following figures correctly represents the reading.
questions.solution:
From screw gauge z.e. $=+0.20 \mathrm{~mm}$ Dia of chalk $=3.5-0.2=3.3 \mathrm{~mm}=0.33 \mathrm{~cm}$
For vernier callipers, z.e. $=-0.05 \mathrm{~cm}$ Thus it must show $0.33-0.05=0.28 \mathrm{~cm}$ so (a) is correct
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
